text.title: pm-certification-changes-2026 Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for pm-certification-changes-2026. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Force-feed the 2026 domain weights (33/41/26) in every system prompt that generates PMP study content; never let the agent infer weights from training data.
rule.rationale: LLMs trained before mid-2025 emit the old weights (42/50/8) confidently; explicit injection is the only reliable fix.

rule.statement: Restrict "Business Environment" to PMI vocabulary only; reject AI-generated content that conflates it with SOC, ITIL, or general business strategy terms.
rule.rationale: Business Environment is an ITIL and SOC term with different meanings; cross-pollination produces incorrect exam answers.

rule.statement: Fetch the current PMI ECO PDF before generating any study plan; do not use cached or inferred task lists.
rule.rationale: ECO layout and task lists change each cycle; stale ECO data produces study plans misaligned with the actual exam.

rule.statement: Cite one sustainability reference per generated answer (GPM P5, ISO 21502, or ISO 14001); do not mix standards within a single answer.
rule.rationale: PMI cites multiple sustainability standards with different scopes; mixing produces inconsistent and potentially incorrect guidance.

rule.statement: Distinguish AI as exam content (what candidates must know about AI in PM) from AI used in studying (what tools candidates use); never conflate the two.
rule.rationale: AI-in-PM is now a testable topic; conflation confuses exam preparation with tool usage.
